name: Shortcut Creator
description: A utility for creating and deploying desktop shortcuts through Microsoft Intune as Win32 applications. Generates .intunewin files, detection scripts, and deployment instructions automatically. Supports URL, file path, and Microsoft Store app shortcuts with custom icons.
keywords: how to deploy desktop shortcut with intune
troubleshoot shortcut not appearing on desktop
install desktop link via win32 app
deploy url link to all users
author: Shehab Noaman

securityCheck.aiSummary: Overall assessment: The codebase implements legitimate Microsoft Intune Graph-based operations (device categorization, reporting, and a device rename workflow). There are no signs of obfuscated code, remote code downloads, credential harvesting, external data exfiltration, or hardcoded secrets. A notable risk is the use of a scheduled task to rename devices on shutdown (Install.ps1), which can be a valid admin tactic but also a potential persistence vector if compromised. Recommend enabling code signing, auditing, least-privilege tokens, and strict governance around startup/shutdown automation. Ensure compatibility with naming policies and test changes in a controlled environment.
